Package com.adrewdev.functional

Class Maybe<T>

java.lang.Object
com.adrewdev.functional.Maybe<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of the contained value
public final class Maybe<T> extends Object
Represents a value that may be present (Some) or absent (None).

This class is a 1:1 port of the TypeScript Maybe type from typescript-functional-extensions. It provides a type-safe way to handle potentially null values without using null directly.

A Maybe is either:

  • Some(value) - contains a non-null value
  • None - represents the absence of a value

Author:
Ported from typescript-functional-extensions
See Also:
  • Optional

  • Method Details

    • from

      public static <T> Maybe<T> from(T value)
      Creates a Maybe from a potentially null value.

      If the value is null, returns None. Otherwise, returns Some(value). This is the safest way to create a Maybe when you're not sure if the value is null.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the value
      Parameters:
      value - the value to wrap, may be null
      Returns:
      Some(value) if value is non-null, None if value is null
      See Also:
      • some(Object)
      • none()

    • some

      public static <T> Maybe<T> some(T value)
      Creates a Maybe containing the given non-null value.

      Use this method when you know the value is non-null and want to explicitly create a Some instance.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the value
      Parameters:
      value - the non-null value to wrap
      Returns:
      a new Maybe containing the value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if value is null
      See Also:
      • from(Object)
      • none()

    • none

      public static <T> Maybe<T> none()
      Returns a Maybe representing the absence of a value.

      This is a singleton - all calls to none() return the same instance.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the Maybe (inferred from context)
      Returns:
      the singleton None instance
      See Also:
      • empty()
      • from(Object)

    • empty

      public static <T> Maybe<T> empty()
      Returns a Maybe representing the absence of a value.

      This is an alias for none() and returns the same singleton instance.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the Maybe (inferred from context)
      Returns:
      the singleton None instance
      See Also:
      • none()

    • tryFirst

      public static <T> Maybe<T> tryFirst(List<T> list)
      Returns a Maybe containing the first element of the list, or None if the list is null or empty.

      Example: 

      
 Maybe<String> first = Maybe.tryFirst(Arrays.asList("a", "b", "c"));
 first.match(
     value -> System.out.println("First: " + value),
     () -> System.out.println("List is empty")
 );
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of elements in the list
      Parameters:
      list - the list to get the first element from, may be null
      Returns:
      a Maybe containing the first element, or None if the list is null or empty
      See Also:
      • tryLast(List)
      • from(Object)

    • tryLast

      public static <T> Maybe<T> tryLast(List<T> list)
      Returns a Maybe containing the last element of the list, or None if the list is null or empty.

      Example: 

      
 Maybe<String> last = Maybe.tryLast(Arrays.asList("a", "b", "c"));
 last.match(
     value -> System.out.println("Last: " + value),
     () -> System.out.println("List is empty")
 );
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of elements in the list
      Parameters:
      list - the list to get the last element from, may be null
      Returns:
      a Maybe containing the last element, or None if the list is null or empty
      See Also:
      • tryFirst(List)
      • from(Object)

    • isSome

      public boolean isSome()
      Returns true if this Maybe contains a value (is Some).
      Returns:
      true if this is Some, false if this is None
      See Also:
      • isNone()
      • isDefined()

    • isNone

      public boolean isNone()
      Returns true if this Maybe represents the absence of a value (is None).
      Returns:
      true if this is None, false if this is Some
      See Also:
      • isSome()
      • isDefined()

    • isDefined

      public boolean isDefined()
      Returns true if this Maybe contains a value (is Some).

      This is an alias for isSome() and returns the same result.

      Returns:
      true if this is Some, false if this is None
      See Also:
      • isSome()
      • isNone()

    • getValueOrThrow

      public T getValueOrThrow()
      Returns the contained value if present, throws NoSuchElementException otherwise.
      Returns:
      the contained value
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if this is None
      See Also:
      • getValueOrThrow(Supplier)
      • getValueOrDefault(Object)

    • getValueOrThrow

      public T getValueOrThrow(Supplier<String> messageSupplier)
      Returns the contained value if present, throws NoSuchElementException with the provided message otherwise.
      Parameters:
      messageSupplier - a supplier of the exception message
      Returns:
      the contained value
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if this is None
      See Also:
      • getValueOrThrow()
      • getValueOrDefault(Object)

    • getValueOrDefault

      public T getValueOrDefault(T defaultValue)
      Returns the contained value if present, or the given default value if None.
      Parameters:
      defaultValue - the default value to return if this is None
      Returns:
      the contained value if present, otherwise the default value
      See Also:
      • getValueOrDefault(Supplier)
      • getValueOrThrow()

    • getValueOrDefault

      public T getValueOrDefault(Supplier<T> defaultSupplier)
      Returns the contained value if present, or the value supplied by the given supplier if None.

      The supplier is only invoked if this is None.

      Parameters:
      defaultSupplier - a supplier of the default value
      Returns:
      the contained value if present, otherwise the supplied default value
      See Also:
      • getValueOrDefault(Object)
      • getValueOrThrow()

    • toOptional

      public Optional<T> toOptional()
      Returns the underlying Optional containing the value.
      Returns:
      the Optional wrapping the value
      See Also:
      • from(Object)

    • map

      public <U> Maybe<U> map(Function<T,U> mapper)
      Transforms the contained value using the given function if present.

      If this is Some, applies the mapper function to the value and wraps the result in a new Maybe. If this is None, returns None without calling the mapper.

      Type Parameters:
      U - the type of the transformed value
      Parameters:
      mapper - a function to apply to the value if present
      Returns:
      a Maybe containing the result of applying the mapper, or None if this is None
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if mapper is null
      See Also:
      • bind(Function)
      • tap(Consumer)

    • bind

      public <U> Maybe<U> bind(Function<T,Maybe<U>> binder)
      Transforms the contained value using the given function that returns a Maybe.

      Also known as "flatMap" or "chain". If this is Some, applies the binder function to the value and returns the resulting Maybe. If this is None, returns None without calling the binder.

      Type Parameters:
      U - the type of the transformed value
      Parameters:
      binder - a function to apply to the value if present, returning a Maybe
      Returns:
      the result of applying the binder, or None if this is None
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if binder is null
      See Also:
      • map(Function)
      • tap(Consumer)

    • tap

      public Maybe<T> tap(Consumer<T> consumer)
      Executes the given consumer with the contained value if present.

      Useful for performing side effects on the value without transforming it. Returns this Maybe for method chaining.

      Parameters:
      consumer - a consumer to execute with the value if present
      Returns:
      this Maybe instance for chaining
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null
      See Also:
      • map(Function)
      • bind(Function)

    • where

      public Maybe<T> where(Predicate<T> predicate)
      Returns this Maybe if it contains a value and the predicate matches, otherwise returns None.

      Also known as "filter". If this is None, returns None without evaluating the predicate.

      Parameters:
      predicate - a predicate to test the value against
      Returns:
      this Maybe if predicate matches, None otherwise
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if predicate is null
      See Also:
      • ensure(Predicate)

    • ensure

      public Maybe<T> ensure(Predicate<T> predicate)
      Returns this Maybe if it contains a value and the predicate matches, otherwise returns None.

      This is an alias for where(java.util.function.Predicate) and behaves identically.

      Parameters:
      predicate - a predicate to test the value against
      Returns:
      this Maybe if predicate matches, None otherwise
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if predicate is null
      See Also:
      • where(java.util.function.Predicate)

    • or

      public Maybe<T> or(T value)
      Returns this Maybe if it contains a value, otherwise returns a Maybe containing the given value.
      Parameters:
      value - the value to return if this is None
      Returns:
      this Maybe if Some, Some(value) if None
      See Also:
      • or(Supplier)
      • or(Maybe)

    • or

      public Maybe<T> or(Supplier<T> valueSupplier)
      Returns this Maybe if it contains a value, otherwise returns a Maybe containing the value supplied by the given supplier.

      The supplier is only invoked if this is None.

      Parameters:
      valueSupplier - a supplier of the fallback value
      Returns:
      this Maybe if Some, Some(supplier.get()) if None
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if valueSupplier is null
      See Also:
      • or(Object)
      • or(Maybe)

    • or

      public Maybe<T> or(Maybe<T> other)
      Returns this Maybe if it contains a value, otherwise returns the given Maybe.
      Parameters:
      other - the Maybe to return if this is None
      Returns:
      this Maybe if Some, other if None
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if other is null
      See Also:
      • orElse(Maybe)
      • or(Object)

    • orElse

      public Maybe<T> orElse(Maybe<T> other)
      Returns this Maybe if it contains a value, otherwise returns the given Maybe.

      This is an alias for or(Maybe) and behaves identically.

      Parameters:
      other - the Maybe to return if this is None
      Returns:
      this Maybe if Some, other if None
      See Also:
      • or(Maybe)

    • match

      public <R> R match(MaybeMatcher<T,R> matcher)
      Performs pattern matching on this Maybe.

      Calls onSome(value) if this is Some, or onNone() if this is None.

      Type Parameters:
      R - the type of the result
      Parameters:
      matcher - a matcher providing callbacks for Some and None cases
      Returns:
      the result of calling the appropriate callback
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if matcher is null
      See Also:
      • MaybeMatcher
      • match(Consumer, Runnable)

    • match

      public void match(Consumer<T> some, Runnable none)
      Performs pattern matching on this Maybe with void callbacks.

      Calls some.accept(value) if this is Some, or none.run() if this is None.

      Parameters:
      some - a consumer to execute with the value if this is Some
      none - a runnable to execute if this is None
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if some or none is null
      See Also:
      • match(MaybeMatcher)

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object obj)
      Compares this Maybe with another object for equality.

      Two Maybes are equal if they are both Some with equal values, or both None.

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      obj - the object to compare with
      Returns:
      true if the Maybes are equal, false otherwise

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code for this Maybe.
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      the hash code of the underlying Optional

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this Maybe.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      "Some{value}" if this is Some, "None{}" if this is None

    • toResult

      public <E> Result<T,E> toResult(E error)
      Converts this Maybe to a Result.

      Returns Success if this Maybe contains a value, or Failure with the provided error if it is empty.

      Example: 

      
 Maybe<String> maybe = Maybe.from("value");
 Result<String, String> result = maybe.toResult("No value provided");
 result.match(
     value -> System.out.println("Success: " + value),
     error -> System.out.println("Error: " + error)
 );
      Type Parameters:
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      error - the error to use if this Maybe is empty
      Returns:
      a Result containing Success with the value, or Failure with the error

    • toResult

      public <E> Result<T,E> toResult(Supplier<E> errorSupplier)
      Converts this Maybe to a Result with lazy error evaluation.

      Returns Success if this Maybe contains a value, or Failure with the provided error from the supplier if it is empty.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      errorSupplier - the supplier that provides the error if this Maybe is empty
      Returns:
      a Result containing Success with the value, or Failure with the error
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if errorSupplier is null

    • toMaybeAsync

      public MaybeAsync<T> toMaybeAsync()
      Converts this Maybe to a MaybeAsync.

      Useful for composing with asynchronous operations.

      Example: 

      
 Maybe<String> maybe = Maybe.from("value");
 MaybeAsync<String> async = maybe.toMaybeAsync();
      Returns:
      a MaybeAsync containing the same value as this Maybe
